data.title: Wabash National Non-GAAP EPS of -$1.17 misses by $0.16, revenue of $303.2M misses by $15.85M
data.description:
• Wabash National press release (WNC): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of -$1.17 misses by $0.16.
  • Revenue of $303.2M (-20.4% Y/Y) misses by $15.85M.
  • Quarterly revenue of $303 million - Softer than expected demand, particularly in our Truck Body business, led to revenue coming in below our guidance range. Parts & Services generated positive revenue growth year-over-year.
  • Total backlog of $837 million ending Q1. Near-term market environment continues to be challenging as the industry remains cautious, though underlying indicators point to pending recovery.
  • Q2-2026 revenue outlook midpoint of $390 million, EPS outlook $(0.50). Market conditions and financials expected to improve throughout 2026.

data.title: Colgate-Palmolive Non-GAAP EPS of $0.97 beats by $0.03, revenue of $5.32B beats by $100M
data.description:
• Colgate-Palmolive press release (CL): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.97 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $5.32B (+8.4% Y/Y) beats by $100M.
  • Net sales increased 8.4%; Organic sales* increased 2.9%, including a 0.6% negative impact from lower private label pet food sales

  • Based on current spot rates and including the estimated impact of tariffs announced and finalized as of April 29, 2026:

    • The Company still expects net sales to be up 2% to 6%, including a low-single-digit positive impact from foreign exchange.
    • The Company still expects organic sales growth to be 1% to 4%. This includes the impact from our exit from the private label pet food business.
    • On a GAAP basis, the Company now expects gross profit margin to be down (versus up previously) and still expects advertising to be up on both a dollar basis and as a percentage of net sales and double-digit earnings per share growth.
    • On a non-GAAP (Base Business) basis, the Company now expects gross profit margin to be down (versus up previously) and still expects advertising to be up on both a dollar basis and as a percentage of net sales; it also still expects low to mid-single-digit earnings per share growth.
  • Shares +1.3% PM.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Exxon Mobil reports Q1 EPS beat of $1.16; revenue nearly in-line at $85.14B, output hits 4.6M boe/day
Moderna jumps after strong Q1 beat driven by international sales
data.description:
Moderna (MRNA) added ~9% in the premarket on Friday after the Massachusetts-based biotech reported better-than-expected Q1 2026 financials, thanks mainly to higher COVID-19 vaccine sales in its international markets. 

The maker of the Spikevax COVID vaccine reported $389M in revenue, indicating ~260% YoY growth and beating the consensus by $153.5M, as the company added $311M in revenue from overseas compared to $78M in its home market.

“Net product sales increased due to higher COVID vaccine sales, primarily in international markets, as a result of deliveries under long-term strategic partnerships with government entities,” the company said.

However, Moderna’s (MRNA) net loss widened ~30% YoY to $1.3B even as its R&D and SG&A expenses declined ~24% YoY and ~18% YoY to $649M and $173M, respectively, as the company reported $0.9M in one-off litigation expenses.

As a result, its cost of sales rose to $955M from $90M in the prior-year period, while $878M of litigation settlement-related expenses expanded third-party royalties.

Moderna (MRNA) reaffirmed its guidance to generate up to 10% revenue growth this year with roughly equal contributions from U.S. and international markets. Meanwhile, after accounting for the $0.9B non-recurring litigation settlement charge, the company raised its cost of sales outlook to nearly $1.8B from about $0.9B projected in February.

data.title: Affiliated Managers Group, Inc. Non-GAAP EPS of $8.23 beats by $0.16, revenue of $496.6M misses by $48.66M
data.description:
• Affiliated Managers Group, Inc. press release (AMG): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $8.23 beats by $0.16.
  • Revenue of $496.6M (flat Y/Y) misses by $48.66M.
  • Record AUM of $882 billion and record positive net client cash flows of more than $22 billion driven by ongoing momentum in alternative strategies
  • Net income (controlling interest) of $110 million, Economic net income (controlling interest) of $225 million
  • Economic EPS of $8.23 increased 58% year-over-year, driven by strong organic growth and disciplined capital allocation
  • Repurchased approximately $186 million in common stock

data.title: Macau gaming revenue rises 5.5% in April, still up double digits for the year
data.description:
Macau gross gaming revenue rose 5.5% year over year in April to 19.9B patacas ($2.46B), according to data from the Gaming Inspection and Coordination Bureau. The GGR tally was down about 12.6% on a sequential basis from the level seen in March, although April is typically considered a slow month for the industry due to the lack of any extended holidays.

For the first four months of 2026, Macau GGR is up 12.1% year over year to 85.8 patacas ($10.6B), which is still down 14.5% from the pre-pandemic period in 2019.

Seaport analyst Vitaly Umansky highlighted that the growth deceleration and increased pressure on margins could lead to casino operators becoming more attuned to fluctuations in expenses and player reinvestment as the year progresses.

Macau casino stocks: Wynn Macau (WYNMF) (WYNMY), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Sands China (SCHYY) (SCHYF), Las Vegas Sands (LVS), and MGM China (MCHVF) (MCHVY). MGM Resorts (MGM), Galaxy Entertainment (GXYEF), SJM Holdings (SJMHF) (SJMHY), Melco Resorts & Entertainment (MLCO), and Studio City International (MSC).

data.title: Westwood outlines West III fundraising through early 2027 as AUM rises to $18.3B
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Westwood Holdings Group (WHG) Q1 2026

Management View

  • “Our AUM grew to $18.3 billion, up from $17.4 billion at year-end 2025,” and “our ETF suite of products surpassed $315 million in combined AUM,” (CEO & Director Brian Casey).

  • “West II closed at over $300 million and West III fundraising is now underway,” and “we completed the sale of Vista Bank, generating a net gain of approximately $2 million,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • “This growth was driven primarily by our energy and real asset strategies, particularly private energy funds and energy-focused ETFs, which more than offset modest declines in U.S. value equity,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • “One major highlight was successfully onboarding our first institutional managed investment solutions client, accounting for over $200 million in gross sales,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • “MBST gained approval from its first major wirehouse, a very important distribution milestone,” and “YLDW... is approaching the $25 million threshold typically required for platform onboarding,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • “Today, we reported total revenues of $25 million for the first quarter of 2026 compared to $27.1 million in the fourth quarter and $23.3 million in the prior year's first quarter,” (CFO Terry Forbes).

  • “Our first quarter income of $0.8 million or $0.09 per share,” and “non-GAAP economic earnings were $2.8 million or $0.31 per share,” (CFO Forbes).

Outlook

  • “We have commenced fundraising for Westwood Energy Secondaries Fund III and its related co-investment fund, which we expect to market through early 2027,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • “Our pipeline remains robust across both value and energy strategies with many new opportunities added during the quarter,” and “we are also initiating SMidCap due diligence with 2 of the largest national consultants,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • “Our SMidCap performance has remained strong and our pipeline of opportunities has grown to over $1 billion,” and “we're optimistic that we will land our next institutional client in the coming months,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • No EPS or revenue guidance was provided in the prepared remarks.

Financial Results

  • “First quarter revenues were lower than the fourth quarter due to lower average AUM as well as fourth quarter recognition of performance fees for the prior year,” (CFO Forbes).

  • “Over the quarter, our assets under management experienced net outflows of $50 million and market appreciation of $0.8 billion,” and “our assets under advisement experienced market appreciation of $48 million and net outflows of $50 million,” (CFO Forbes).

  • “Our financial position continues to be solid with cash and liquid investments at quarter end totaling $34.2 million and a debt-free balance sheet,” (CFO Forbes).

  • “Our Board of Directors approved a regular cash dividend of $0.15 per common share payable on July 1, 2026, to stockholders of record on June 1, 2026,” (CFO Forbes).

Q&A

  • No analyst Q&A session was included in this transcript: “I am showing no questions at this time,” (Operator).

Sentiment Analysis

  • Neutral: With no Q&A, analyst tone was not observable in the current-quarter transcript.

  • Slightly positive: Management emphasized scaling initiatives and distribution catalysts, including “we are confident in our ability to capitalize on the opportunities ahead,” (CEO & Director Brian Casey), and highlighted near-term distribution activation: “we're excited to see MBST go fully live tomorrow across one of the major wires,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • Compared with Q4 2025, the prepared-remarks tone remained constructive, while Q4 included explicit disappointment on flows: “we want to acknowledge the outflows in the fourth quarter, which were disappointing,” (Chief Executive Officer Brian Casey).

Quarter-over-quarter Comparison

  • AUM moved from “$17.4 billion” at Q4 2025 quarter-end to “$18.3 billion” at Q1 2026 quarter-end, while management’s mix narrative sharpened toward private energy and real assets: “private fund AUM was the largest contributor,” and “client allocations are shifting toward income-oriented real asset and private market solutions,” (CEO & Director Brian Casey).

  • ETF messaging advanced from Q4’s scale milestone (“our ETF franchise now exceeds $200 million,” (Chief Executive Officer Brian Casey)) to Q1’s distribution unlocks, including “MBST gained approval from its first major wirehouse,” and “higher trading volumes and growing AUM,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • Q4 commentary centered on large-cap value outflows concentration (“more than 80% of the outflows were from our large-cap value product,” (Chief Executive Officer Casey)), while Q1 emphasized moderation: “the pace of decline moderated during the quarter,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • The dividend was reiterated at “$0.15 per common share,” with the payment date moving from “April 1, 2026” (Q4) to “July 1, 2026” (Q1) (CFO Terry Forbes; Chief Executive Officer Brian Casey in Q4).

Risks and Concerns

  • “Traditional U.S. value equity strategies remain under pressure,” (CEO & Director Brian Casey), alongside market-volatility drivers: “military actions by the United States and Israel against Iran drove oil prices significantly higher in March,” (CEO & Director Casey).

  • Management framed mitigation through diversification and distribution: “this growth was structural in nature rather than market dependent,” (CEO & Director Casey), and noted platform scaling efforts: “we have added team members to our private capital operations team and implemented a new AI-driven technology tool to streamline key operational processes,” (CEO & Director Casey).

Final Takeaway

Westwood management described Q1 2026 as a quarter where AUM increased to $18.3 billion alongside continued scaling in ETFs and private energy secondaries. Management highlighted distribution catalysts—wirehouse approval and a “fully live” rollout for MBST, and a platform-onboarding threshold for YLDW—while positioning Westwood Energy Secondaries Fund III fundraising “through early 2027.” The CFO reported $25 million of revenue, $0.09 per share of income, $34.2 million of cash and liquid investments with no debt, and a $0.15 per share dividend scheduled for July 1, 2026.
